steps.title: Match corresponding angles
steps.content: Line $k$ is the transversal. From the figure, angles $w$ and $y$ are corresponding angles on lines $r$ and $s$.

steps.title: Use the converse
steps.content: If corresponding angles are congruent, then the two lines cut by the transversal are parallel. Since $w=160$, the added statement $y=160$ would make $w$ and $y$ congruent.

steps.title: Check the other conditions
steps.content: The condition $x=20$ follows from $w=160$ at the same intersection, so it gives no information about line $s$. The condition $w+y=180$ would make $y=20$, and $y+z=180$ can describe a linear pair on line $s$ without proving the lines are parallel. The answer is B.
